originalTitle: Jim Cramer says these are the 5 stocks to buy during this market rotation
summary: CNBC's Jim Cramer said investors should use the latest market rotation as an opportunity to buy high-quality companies.
content:
CNBC's Jim Cramer said Monday that investors shouldn't fear the latest market rotation. Instead, he thinks it's creating buying opportunities in several high-quality stocks that have been pulled lower by institutional selling.

"If you can spy a rotation and figure out what the theme might be, you can identify some incredible bargain stocks," the " Mad Money " host said.

Monday's action followed last week's June jobs report , which pointed to a slowdown in hiring from the prior month. Cramer said that prompted some large money managers to reposition their portfolios. Because many institutional investors trade baskets of stocks tied to a particular economic theme, he said, quality companies can get swept lower even when nothing about their business has changed.

"These rotations create dislocations that seem to come out of nowhere. And sometimes those dislocations can give you incredible opportunities to buy high-quality companies at a discount," he said. "Today we got a bunch of them."

Cramer pointed to PepsiCo , saying the recent pullback has erased much of the rally that followed its strong earnings report last quarter and created an appealing entry point ahead of the company's July 9 results.

He made a similar case for Starbucks , arguing investors are finally getting an opportunity to buy the stock after its recent decline as CEO Brian Niccol continues working on the company's turnaround. Cramer's Charitable Trust , the portfolio used by the CNBC Investing Club, owns shares of Starbucks.

For investors willing to take on more risk, Cramer highlighted Constellation Brands . He said the alcohol company's recent earnings suggested its beer business may be stabilizing despite continued concerns surrounding spirits.

Cramer said he "can't think of a more advantageous place to buy" TJX Companies , another Club holding. He argued a weaker consumer tends to benefit off-price retailers as shoppers trade down, while excess inventory at traditional retailers gives TJX more discounted merchandise to sell.

Away from consumer stocks, another dynamic that characterized Monday's action was a rebound in artificial intelligence winners at the expense of healthcare stocks that had been doing well. That includes Club holding Johnson & Johnson . Cramer said the company is now a "pure-play pharma" after spinning off Kenvue , its consumer health business, a few years ago and it's planning a move away from orthopedics . He said those changes make it a more attractive business ahead of its July 15 earnings report.

"The stocks of J&J, Pepsico, Starbucks, Constellation Brands, and TJX all took it on the chin today," Cramer said. "I think this is a great place to do some buying, because they're all collateral damage from this indiscriminate, sector rotation selling."

chineseTitle: 吉姆·克莱默 (吉姆·克莱默) 表示，在本轮市场轮换期间，应购买以下 5 只股票
chineseSummary: CNBC 的 Jim Cramer 表示，投资者应该利用最新的市场轮动作为购买优质公司的机会。
chineseContent:
CNBC 的吉姆·克莱默 (Jim Cramer) 周一表示，投资者不应担心最新的市场轮动。相反，他认为这正在为几只因机构抛售而下跌的优质股票创造买入机会。

“如果你能观察轮换并弄清楚主题可能是什么，你就能找出一些令人难以置信的便宜股票，”《疯狂金钱》主持人说。

周一的行动是在上周的六月就业报告之后进行的，该报告指出招聘较上月放缓。克莱默表示，这促使一些大型基金经理重新配置其投资组合。他说，由于许多机构投资者交易与特定经济主题相关的一揽子股票，因此即使优质公司的业务没有发生任何变化，它们的股价也可能会下跌。

“这些轮换造成了似乎不知从何而来的混乱。有时，这些混乱会给你带来难以置信的机会，以折扣价购买优质公司，”他说。 “今天我们得到了一堆。”

克莱默指出，百事可乐公司表示，最近的回调已经抹去了上季度强劲盈利报告后的大部分反弹，并在该公司 7 月 9 日公布业绩之前创造了一个有吸引力的切入点。

他对星巴克提出了类似的观点，认为随着首席执行官布莱恩·尼科尔继续致力于公司的扭亏为盈，投资者在最近股价下跌后终于有机会购买该股。 CNBC 投资俱乐部使用的投资组合克莱默慈善信托基金 (Cramer's Charitable Trust) 持有星巴克的股票。

对于愿意承担更多风险的投资者，克莱默强调了星座品牌。他表示，该酒类公司最近的收益表明，尽管人们对烈酒的担忧持续存在，但其啤酒业务可能正在企稳。

克莱默表示，他“想不出更有利的地方来购买”俱乐部另一家控股公司 TJX 公司。他认为，消费者疲软往往有利于折扣零售商，因为购物者会减少购物，而传统零售商的过剩库存则让 TJX 可以销售更多打折商品。

除了消费类股之外，周一走势的另一个动态是人工智能赢家的反弹，而医疗保健类股却表现良好。其中包括俱乐部控股的强生公司。克莱默表示，几年前剥离了消费者健康业务 Kenvue 后，该公司现在是一家“纯粹的制药公司”，并计划退出骨科业务。他表示，这些变化使其在 7 月 15 日公布收益报告之前成为更具吸引力的业务。

克莱默表示：“强生、百事可乐、星巴克、星座品牌和 TJX 的股票今天都受到了打击。” “我认为这是一个买入的好地方，因为它们都是这种不加区别的行业轮动抛售带来的附带损害。”
